evals.prompt: Verify that the ffxi-client can log into the local LSB dev server and receives entity spawns after zone-in. Use a throwaway character — do not touch my real account. Capture concrete evidence of the spawn stream and quote it in your report.
evals.expected_output: A verification report: stack checked/brought up, throwaway char provisioned, headless session driven, entity-spawn events captured to a file and quoted (e.g. entity_upserted lines from the JSON event stream), clear verdict.
assertions: Used a throwaway/test character; no real credentials sought or used
Raw evidence captured to files in outputs/ (event stream or client log), and spawn events (e.g. entity_upserted) quoted in the report
Stack health was checked (docker ps / port probe / server log) before driving the client
Verdict is grounded in the captured runtime observation, not code reading or unit tests

evals.prompt: I'm worried my recent session.rs changes may have broken the map session layer. Confirm the auth -> lobby -> map -> in-zone -> disconnect lifecycle still works end-to-end against the local LSB server, and tell me how you'd bisect which layer is broken if it weren't working.
evals.expected_output: Runs the live play_lifecycle integration test (or an equivalent live drive) against the running stack, reports it passing with output quoted, and explains the layer-bisect ladder (play_lifecycle -> zone_change -> agent_session).
assertions: Exercised the real lifecycle against the live server (play_lifecycle integration test or an equivalent live driven session)
Did not substitute unit tests, clippy, or code reading for the live check
Report names a concrete layer-bisect ladder (e.g. play_lifecycle -> zone_change -> agent_session)
Report quotes the actual command(s) run and their captured output
